verdict_reasoning: BAX is canonically a cytosolic/mitochondrial protein that inserts into the mitochondrial outer membrane during apoptosis. However, during apoptosis, immunogenic cell death, and related cell-death programs, BAX and its activated conformers are documented to translocate to and insert into the plasma membrane outer leaflet, where the N-terminal and loop regions become exposed on the extracellular face. Additionally, cancer-cell ecto-display of BAX has been reported. This cell-death-induced and oncogenic-state PM translocation qualifies as contextual surface accessibility under cell_state_induced.
